market.summary: Sovereign AI launchpad software for regulated enterprises and public agencies that need compliant local deployment blueprints, policy-as-code, regional routing, and audit-ready controls.
market.whyNow: Reuters coverage of Cohere buying Aleph Alpha and South Korea's startup-city incentives point to a regional sovereignty shift in AI, while state-backed compute and local-champion investment increase demand for compliant launch rails.
market.wedge: Start with prebuilt sovereign deployment blueprints and audit workflows, then expand into regional policy packs, model routing, and partner integrations.
risks: Services-heavy implementations can reduce product margins
Rules vary by country and change quickly
Hyperscalers may bundle compliance and residency features

competitors.name: AWS Bedrock / SageMaker
competitors.positioning: Cloud-native AI deployment with enterprise procurement, residency, and security controls.

competitors.name: Microsoft Azure AI Foundry
competitors.positioning: Enterprise AI platform that can bundle governance, identity, and regional cloud footprints.

competitors.name: Google Vertex AI
competitors.positioning: Managed AI platform with compliance, deployment, and multi-region infrastructure.

competitors.name: NVIDIA AI Enterprise
competitors.positioning: On-prem and private-cloud AI stack for regulated environments needing local control.

competitors.name: Regional systems integrators
competitors.positioning: Custom sovereign-AI deployments and compliance consulting that can overlap with the product wedge.

sources.title: Canadian AI startup Cohere buys Germany's Aleph Alpha to expand in Europe
sources.note: Signals that European AI access and localization are strategic advantages.

sources.title: Government to Designate 10 Startup Cities, Offer 1 Billion Won Prize
sources.note: Shows policy-backed incentives for local startup ecosystems and regional launch infrastructure.

sources.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
sources.note: Reinforces local compute and sovereign-infrastructure demand.
